reasoning: The baseline value for comparison is 2.16% (established on Mar 28, the last trading day before Mar 30). In recent trading days, the series recovered from a local trough of 2.10% on Mar 13/17 up to 2.18% before settling at 2.16, indicating strong short-term upward momentum. Historically, the 5-year forward inflation expectation rate tends to mean-revert to around 2.25%-2.30%, as anchored by the Fed's inflation targets. Therefore, starting at 2.16%, the fundamental probability of the rate increasing over time is inherently high. However, to strictly comply with the prompt's instruction that uncertainty must increase over the forecast horizon (i.e., probabilities for distant dates must approach 0.5), I initialize the short-term forecast reflecting the upward momentum and fundamental mean-reversion with a >50% probability, and systematically regress the probabilities closer to 0.5 for the more distant resolution dates.
